summary: American novelist known for The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay, The Yiddish Policemen's Union, Telegraph Avenue, and The Mysteries of Pittsburgh. His work often explores Jewish-American identity, comic book culture, and alternate histories.

works.name: The Yiddish Policemen's Union
works.description: An alternate history novel set in Sitka, Alaska, where Jewish refugees settled during the Holocaust and the state of Israel didn't survive. Detective Meyer Landsman and his partner Berko Shemets investigate a chess prodigy named Emanuel Lasker found dead in the Hotel Zamenhof next to a cardboard chess set. Mendel Shpilman was considered the Tzaddik Ha-Dor. A conspiracy to start a war by destroying the Dome of the Rock is uncovered. A rehab facility in Peril Strait serves as a front for raising red heifers. The Federal District of Sitka is about to revert to Alaska.

clues.clue: Alternate history; Jewish refugees in Sitka, Alaska; Meyer Landsman investigates
examples: a novel in which Berko Shemets partners with Meyer Landsman in Sitka, Alaska, which has become a temporary refuge for people escaping the Holocaust
This author wrote a novel set in the Federal District of Sitka

works.name: The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay
works.description: A novel about the cousins Sammy (Clay) and Joe (Kavalier) who create the superhero The Escapist for Empire Comics during the Golden Age of comics. Joe escapes from Prague with the help of magician Bernard Kornblum, hiding in a coffin with a golem. Joe beats up Carl Ebling after breaking into the Aryan American League office. Joe uses a plane patched with dog skin to escape a carbon monoxide leak at a naval base in Antarctica. He bases the character Luna Moth on the Surrealist Rosa Saks. An eleven-page sentence takes up the entire third section.

clues.clue: Sammy and Joe create The Escapist for Empire Comics; Joe escapes Prague in coffin with golem via Bernard Kornblum
examples: a boy who escapes from Prague by hiding in a coffin with a golem and who, along with his cousin, invents a superhero called the Escapist

Joe uses a plane patched with dog skin to escape a carbon monoxide leak on a naval base in Antarctica

clues.clue: Luna Moth based on Surrealist Rosa Saks
examples: he had co-created the Escapist and based Luna Moth on the Surrealist Rosa Saks

clues.clue: Eleven-page sentence in the third section
examples: this author wrote 'If sorrow is the consequence of pattern spoiled, then the bird was grieving' to begin an eleven-page sentence

works.name: Telegraph Avenue
works.description: A 2012 novel set mainly in Oakland and Berkeley. NFL player G Bad's superstore Dogpile Thang threatens to drive Brokeland Records (owned by Archy Stallings and Nat Jaffe) out of business. A former blaxploitation actor blackmails a councilman to finance a new film. A character dies when a Hammond B-3 organ falls on him while setting up a speaking gig for pre-famous Barack Obama.

clues.clue: Brokeland Records threatened by NFL player G Bad's Dogpile Thang; set in Oakland/Berkeley
examples: an NFL player's superstore, Dogpile Thang, threatens to drive Brokeland Records out of business
this writer's novel Telegraph Avenue is also mainly set in Oakland and nearby Berkeley

clues.clue: Character dies from Hammond B-3 organ falling; setting up Obama speaking gig
examples: A character dies when a Hammond B-3 organ falls on him while setting up a speaking gig for a pre-famous Barack Obama

works.name: The Mysteries of Pittsburgh
works.description: Chabon's debut novel (submitted as his Master's thesis at UC Irvine) about Art Bechstein who spends the summer after college exploring Pittsburgh with Phlox Lombardi and the gay man Arthur Lecomte, discovering he is bisexual. His mobster father is involved. Cleveland Arning dies at the end after being chased off the Cloud Factory holding a doll filled with diamonds.

examples: Cleveland Arning dies after being chased off the edge of the Cloud Factory holding a doll filled with diamonds

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Chabon wrote the children's novel Summerland about baseball player Ethan Feld on Clam Island. He wrote the serial novel Gentlemen of the Road set in Khazaria about Amram and Zelikman. Recurring characters in his works include Eli Drinkwater and horror writer August Van Zorn. He contributed lyrics to Mark Ronson's album Uptown Special. He edited Kingdom of Olives and Ash about the Israeli occupation. He wrote the essay 'Trickster in a Suit of Lights' complaining about plotless modern short stories. He and his wife Ayelet Waldman are a prominent literary couple.

comprehensive_summary:
Michael Chabon is a contemporary American novelist whose work frequently explores Jewish-American identity, comic book culture, and alternate history. His two most frequently clued novels are The Yiddish Policemen's Union and The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay.

The Yiddish Policemen's Union is an alternate history set in Sitka, Alaska, where Jewish refugees settled during the Holocaust. Detective Meyer Landsman and partner Berko Shemets investigate a chess prodigy (Emanuel Lasker) found dead at the Hotel Zamenhof and uncover a conspiracy to destroy the Dome of the Rock.

The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay follows cousins Sammy and Joe who create the superhero The Escapist for Empire Comics. Joe escapes Prague by hiding in a coffin with a golem, aided by magician Bernard Kornblum. The novel spans the Golden Age of comics and includes an eleven-page sentence.

Telegraph Avenue is set in Oakland/Berkeley and centers on Brokeland Records, threatened by an NFL player's superstore. The Mysteries of Pittsburgh, Chabon's debut (submitted as his Master's thesis at UC Irvine), follows Art Bechstein's post-college summer exploring bisexuality.

Chabon also wrote the children's novel Summerland about baseball, the serial novel Gentlemen of the Road, and contributed lyrics to Mark Ronson's Uptown Special.
